artPrompt: The dramatic moment a sly carny's hands sweep three battered walnut shells across a rigged table in motion-blurred foreshortening, a tiny glowing pea palmed in his cuff catching hot-gold light. Three creature-silhouette fates flicker beneath the shells; tattered red tents and neon blaze behind through smoky carnival haze, ember sparks streaking, sleight-of-hand kinetic deceit, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Shell Game
text: Choose three creatures you don't control. Behind the table, the carny shuffles their fates. Roll a six-sided die: on 1 or 2, gain control of the first until end of turn; on 3 or 4, the second; on 5 or 6, the third. Then it attacks if able. (The pea was under your sleeve the whole time.)
flavorText: Keep your eye on the shells. Don't look at the carny's other hand. Definitely don't.
